title_en: Taharah According to the Mu'tamad Hanbali Position
title_id: Taharah Menurut Pendapat Mu'tamad Mazhab Hanbali
content_en:
Al-Mardawi's treatment of taharah in Al-Insaf is distinguished by his systematic effort to resolve the many internal disagreements within the Hanbali school on questions of ritual purity. The Hanbali tradition, having developed through multiple chains of transmission from Imam Ahmad and his diverse students, accumulated a range of opinions on purity that al-Mardawi felt compelled to evaluate and rank.

On water, the Hanbali school broadly accepts the same categories as other schools: pure and purifying water (tahur), pure but not purifying water, and impure water. Where the Hanbali school shows distinctive features is in certain details: for example, the Hanbali position on the purification of a utensil licked by a dog requires seven washings, one with earth, in agreement with the Shafi'i school and in contrast to the Maliki and Hanafi positions. Al-Mardawi traces the evidential basis for the Hanbali position and confirms it as the mu'tamad.

On the question of whether wudu is broken by touching a woman with desire, the Hanbali school has a position that distinguishes it from others. Imam Ahmad held that touching a woman with desire (or even without desire, according to some transmissions) breaks wudu. Al-Mardawi examines the various transmitted opinions of Ahmad on this question — a question complicated by multiple apparently contradictory statements — and identifies the mu'tamad position: that mere touching without desire does not break wudu, but touching with desire in a manner that would typically produce pleasure does break it.

On the five conditions of wudu — water reaching the required body parts, proper sequence (tartib), continuity (muwalah), rubbing (dalk) in the view that holds it obligatory, and intention — the Hanbali school agrees with the Shafi'is on sequence and continuity being obligatory, in contrast to the Hanafis and Malikis who differ on these points. Al-Mardawi confirms this as the stronger position within the school.

The ghusl chapter presents its own internal discussions, particularly on what triggers the obligation of ghusl. The Hanbali school requires ghusl when the two circumcised parts meet, even without ejaculation — a position shared with the Shafi'is and based on the hadis of Abu Hurayra. Al-Mardawi discusses the occasional ambiguity in Ahmad's transmitted statements on this and the strong majority position within the school, which he confirms as the mu'tamad.

Tayammum receives detailed treatment, with al-Mardawi addressing the conditions of valid dry ablution: absence of sufficient water within the relevant distance, the use of clean earth or soil, and the performance covering the face and both arms to the elbows. He addresses the Hanbali position that tayammum breaks upon the return of the ability to use water, even before the prayer time ends.
content_id:
Pembahasan al-Mardawi tentang taharah dalam Al-Insaf dibedakan oleh upayanya yang sistematis untuk menyelesaikan banyak perbedaan pendapat internal dalam mazhab Hanbali mengenai persoalan bersuci. Tradisi Hanbali, yang berkembang melalui berbagai rantai transmisi dari Imam Ahmad dan murid-muridnya yang beragam, mengakumulasi berbagai pendapat tentang kesucian yang al-Mardawi merasa perlu untuk dievaluasi dan diberi peringkat.

Mengenai air, mazhab Hanbali secara luas menerima kategori yang sama seperti mazhab-mazhab lain: air suci dan menyucikan (tahur), air suci tetapi tidak menyucikan, dan air najis. Di mana mazhab Hanbali menunjukkan ciri-ciri yang khas adalah pada detail-detail tertentu: misalnya, posisi Hanbali tentang penyucian bejana yang dijilat anjing memerlukan tujuh kali pencucian, satu kali dengan tanah, sesuai dengan mazhab Syafi'i dan berbeda dari posisi Maliki dan Hanafi. Al-Mardawi menelusuri dasar evidensial posisi Hanbali dan mengkonfirmasinya sebagai mu'tamad.

Mengenai persoalan apakah wudu batal dengan menyentuh perempuan dengan syahwat, mazhab Hanbali memiliki posisi yang membedakannya dari mazhab-mazhab lain. Imam Ahmad berpendapat bahwa menyentuh perempuan dengan syahwat (atau bahkan tanpa syahwat, menurut beberapa riwayat) membatalkan wudu. Al-Mardawi memeriksa berbagai pendapat Ahmad yang diriwayatkan mengenai persoalan ini — suatu persoalan yang dipersulit oleh beberapa pernyataan yang tampaknya saling bertentangan — dan mengidentifikasi posisi mu'tamad: bahwa sekadar menyentuh tanpa syahwat tidak membatalkan wudu, tetapi menyentuh dengan syahwat dengan cara yang biasanya menimbulkan kenikmatan memang membatalkannya.

Mengenai lima syarat wudu — air menjangkau anggota badan yang dipersyaratkan, urutan yang benar (tartib), kesinambungan (muwalah), menggosok (dalk) menurut pandangan yang mewajibkannya, dan niat — mazhab Hanbali sependapat dengan Syafi'i bahwa urutan dan kesinambungan adalah wajib, berbeda dari Hanafi dan Maliki yang berbeda pendapat dalam hal-hal ini. Al-Mardawi mengkonfirmasi ini sebagai posisi yang lebih kuat dalam mazhab.

Bab ghusl menghadirkan diskusi internalnya sendiri, terutama tentang apa yang mewajibkan ghusl. Mazhab Hanbali mewajibkan ghusl ketika dua bagian yang dikhitan bertemu, meskipun tanpa inzal — suatu posisi yang dianut bersama dengan Syafi'i dan berdasarkan hadis Abu Hurayra. Al-Mardawi membahas ambiguitas yang kadang muncul dalam pernyataan-pernyataan Ahmad yang diriwayatkan tentang hal ini dan posisi mayoritas yang kuat dalam mazhab, yang ia konfirmasi sebagai mu'tamad.

Tayammum mendapat pembahasan yang terperinci, dengan al-Mardawi membahas syarat-syarat tayammum yang sah: ketiadaan air yang cukup dalam jarak yang relevan, penggunaan tanah atau debu yang bersih, dan pelaksanaannya yang mencakup wajah dan kedua lengan hingga siku. Beliau membahas posisi Hanbali bahwa tayammum batal ketika kemampuan menggunakan air kembali, meskipun waktu salat belum berakhir.
content_ar: Al-Mardawi's treatment of الطهارة in Al-Insaf is distinguished by his systematic effort to resolve the many internal disagreements within the الحنبلي school on questions of ritual purity. The الحنبلي التراث, having developed through multiple chains of transmission from الإمام أحمد and his diverse students, accumulated a range of opinions on purity that al-Mardawi felt compelled to evaluate and rank. On water, the الحنبلي school broadly accepts the same categories as other schools: pure and purifying water (tahur), pure but not purifying water, and impure water. Where the الحنبلي school shows distinctive features is in certain details: على سبيل المثال, the الحنبلي position on the purification of a utensil licked by a dog requires seven washings, one with earth, in agreement with the الشافعي school and in contrast to the المالكي and الحنفي positions. Al-Mardawi traces the evidential basis for the الحنبلي position and confirms it as the mu'tamad. On the question of whether الوضوء is broken by touching a woman with desire, the الحنبلي school has a position that distinguishes it from others. الإمام أحمد held that touching a woman with desire (or even without desire, وفقاً لـ some transmissions) breaks الوضوء. Al-Mardawi examines the various transmitted opinions of أحمد on this question — a question complicated by multiple apparently contradictory statements — and identifies the mu'tamad position: that mere touching without desire does not break الوضوء, but touching with desire in a manner that would typically produce pleasure does break it. On the five conditions of الوضوء — water reaching the required الجسد parts, proper sequence (tartib), continuity (muwalah), rubbing (dalk) in the view that holds it obligatory, and intention — the الحنبلي school agrees with the Shafi'is on sequence and continuity being obligatory, in contrast to the Hanafis and Malikis who differ on these points. Al-Mardawi confirms this as the stronger position within the school. The الغسل الباب presents its own internal discussions, particularly on what triggers the الالتزام of الغسل. The الحنبلي school requires الغسل عندما the two circumcised parts meet, even without ejaculation — a position shared with the Shafi'is and based on the hadis of Abu Hurayra. Al-Mardawi discusses the occasional ambiguity in أحمد's transmitted statements on this and the strong majority position within the school, which he confirms as the mu'tamad. التيمم receives detailed treatment, with al-Mardawi addressing the conditions of valid dry ablution: absence of sufficient water within the relevant distance, the use of clean earth or soil, and the performance covering the face and both arms to the elbows. He addresses the الحنبلي position that التيمم breaks upon the return of the ability to use water, even قبل the الصلاة time ends.
